sglang.title: [diffusion] optimize LTX2.3 CFG/SP paths
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR keeps the LTX2.3 changes that have real pipeline support:

keeps packed video/audio latents local across CFG-parallel batch broadcast, avoiding Python object broadcast of rank0 CUDA tensors for those fields
changes LTX2 auto parallelism policy so 4-GPU and 8-GPU auto modes use pure S

sglang.title: Add native Exa-backed web_search support
sglang.author: jonah-berman
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Adds native web_search support for GPT-OSS/SGLang Responses workflows, backed by Exa when EXA_API_KEY is configured on the SGLang server.
This keeps the user-facing abstraction provider-neutral (web_search) while using Exa as the default native backend for the existing hosted browser tool pa

sglang.title: Fix DeepSeek V4 PP HiCache SWA allocation and layer mapping
sglang.author: 1e4ves
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
DeepSeek V4 + HiCache can fail under pipeline parallelism because the SWA KVpool is allocated with the global model layer count, while each PP stage only owns a local layer slice. In PP + HiCache runs this leads to invalid HiCache state construction on later PP stages and can fail during

sglang.title: hisparse: support NIXL DRAM KV destinations for HiSparse
sglang.author: ishandhanani
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR makes the NIXL disaggregation path work with HiSparse decode KV destinations that live in host memory, and exposes the HiSparse swap-in CUDA launch block size through --hisparse-config.
The original failure mode was that decode-side HiSparse advertises host-backed KV transfer buffers

Modifications

Part 2 of a 4-PR split of #27944 (MiniMax-M3). Adds the MiniMax sparse KV cache pool and its HiCache/hybrid-pool + configurator wiring.
Depends on #28712 (Split 1/4). Land Split 1 first, then rebase this on main — it imports is_minimax_sparse / get_minimax_sparse_* from model_config and store_kv_ind

sglang.title: Support JIT fused A GEMM (MLA down projection) and support GLM-5 hidden size, SM120
sglang.author: b8zhong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Modifications

Adopt this kernel to JIT, to support GLM-5 hidden size (6144)
Support SM120, by decreasing the number of stages to fit the SM120 shared memory (99 KB) vs 228 KB on Hopper/Blackwell (since this kernel uses mma.sync for latency instead of tensor core, it's fully compatible)
Rewrite this

sglang.author: b8zhong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Note: to completely revert the affects of this PR, we can set SGLANG_DISABLE_DSA_INDEXER_FUSION=1
Changes


Fuse the key projection and the head-gate projection into a single matmul
instead of two.


Fuse the query path — rope, optional Hadamard, fp8 quantization, and
head-gate scaling — into one ke

sglang.title: (perf): Shard Kimi-K2.5 Eagle3 draft fc + symm-mem AG
sglang.author: kpham-sgl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The Kimi-K2.x EAGLE3 spec-decode path ran two NCCL all-gathers on the hot path:
the draft fc projection gather and the vocab-parallel LM-head logits gather
(hit by both draft sampling and target verify). This PR shards the fc weight
and replaces both NCCL all-gathers with a single symmetric-

sglang.title: [Spec] Capture DFLASH draft greedy sampling inside the draft decode cuda graph
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt: DFLASH's draft model has no LM head of its own; it borrows the target model's lm_head to greedily sample the proposed draft tokens. That projection (matmul + argmax) ran eagerly in the worker between the draft and verify decode cuda graphs, so it was an extra launch in the gap and was not counted by

sglang.title: Fix SWA cache loc slicing for all attention backends
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Move swa_out_cache_loc slicing into KVWriteLoc.__post_init__ so it applies to all attention backends, not just TRT-LLM MHA.
swa_out_cache_loc is computed once at metadata-init time from the full (possibly padded) out_cache_loc. Piecewise CUDA graphs later narrow out_cache_loc to real_num_to

sglang.title: [diffusion] fix --warmup silently downgrading server-based warmup to request mode
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
sglang serve --warmup is meant to use server-based warmup — the serve entrypoint injects a default warmup_mode="server" "for production". But it silently downgrades to request-based warmup, and combined with --warmup-resolutions it disables warmup entirely. The --warmup flag can thus effe

sglang.title: Fix DP-attention SHM feature finalization race
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Under DP-attention, the broadcast source rank returns with its original
objects while peer ranks may still be unpickling ShmPointerMMData
(shm_open).  Previously the SHM barrier only covered the non-DP-attention
path — under DP-attention no synchronization was performed, so
materialize()

sglang.title: Fix EAGLE draft hidden dim extraction and centralize spec helpers
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Consolidate hidden-state width/dtype resolution for EAGLE speculative decoding into two centralized helpers in eagle_utils.py:

get_draft_input_from_target_hidden_dim: derives the target hidden states width from config (handles EAGLE3 aux mode). Replaces reading from fc.in_features which is

to Fp8LinearMethod and RoutedExperts layers to Fp8MoEMethod, so a
Rou

vllm.title: Fixed chunked embedding aggregation with request-id metadata
vllm.author: taneem-ibrahim
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Chunked embedding aggregation currently recovers prompt/chunk identity by parsing generated request IDs such as ...-prompt-{i}-chunk-{j}. That makes aggregation depend on request ID string structure, even though callers can influence the base request ID through X-Request-Id. If a user-provid

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2][Spec Decode] Use fp32 uniform threshold for acceptance
vllm.author: TheEpicDolphin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Context
Currently in MRV2 we do an FP64 uniform random draw in the rejection sample kernel. This precision level is higher than necessary.
This PR
Switch to FP32 uniform random draw, similar to what is done in gumbel sampling.

      --backend openai-chat \
